title: Child-Priority Replacement Strategy, Filter Distribution Equity, and Education Gaps
summary: Council pushes for prioritizing lead service line replacements at homes with children; Commissioner explains current phase covers 2,900 private-side completions via grant while citywide plan uses equity scoring for child population density. Filter distribution debate: WIC collaboration mentioned, Brita pitcher filters widely rejected (too large, taste complaints, many discarded); Council member argues city should revisit filter type since EPA requires lead-reduction certification, not a specific product. Commissioner attends Lead Safe CNY Coalition monthly meetings and personally delivers filters to residents lacking transportation. Council pushes for consistent education: bold-letter mailings, community centers, libraries, school partnerships. Commissioner reports distributing 6,000+ Brita filters in 2024 to all K-grade-1 students in city and charter schools. Ongoing debate about balancing street-by-street efficiency with needs-based targeting of vulnerable households.

political_signal.description: Council pressing for child-focused prioritization, better filter options, and more proactive education distribution through community channels

processing_notes: Lead Safe CNY Coalition = community organization focused on lead exposure prevention; commissioner attends monthly meetings
'Latoya' mentioned as coalition contact -- not in entity registry
EPA filter requirement: debate over whether specific Brita pitcher is required vs any certified lead-reduction filter
Segment 4 of 7 from Public Works and Infrastructure Committee Meeting, January 20th 2026
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00052 -- linked, not skipped
